authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal civil-rights authority for ADA Title III enforcement, and the lodging reservations section directly governs accessible-room reservations, accessible-feature descriptions, reservation-system blocking, and equal-access handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: reservation intake, accessible-room communication, and booking-record verification

authoritative_sources.title: ADA Guide for Places of Lodging: Serving Guests Who Are Blind or Who Have Low Vision
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ lodging guidance is an official hospitality-specific interpretation aid for effective communication, alternate-format materials, service-animal handling, and wayfinding support during a guest stay.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: during-stay communication, alternate-format information handling, and accommodation-aware guest messaging

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official short-term-lodging compliance guide defines total-price disclosure, mandatory-fee treatment, truthful fee descriptions, and the timing of final payment disclosures for hotels, vacation rentals, platforms, and travel agents.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: rate, fee, and booking communication; amenity or resort-fee complaints; and outage-related price-disclosure handling
used_for: conflict handling between public baseline and tenant fee scripts

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance and is authoritative for merchant-versus-issuer boundaries relevant to folio disputes, duplicate charges, and refund-not-posted complaints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: folio mismatch, duplicate charge handling, refund status communication, and issuer handoff packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Travel-associated Outbreaks as a Public Health Issue
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the primary U.S. public-health authority, and this accommodation-specific outbreak guidance directly addresses guest-list requests, notifying incoming or past guests, and escalation behavior after suspected exposure events at accommodations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: safety-sensitive disruption communication, outbreak notification handling, and public-health escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity and account-use signals that affect refund reroutes, reservation changes, guest-profile updates, and account-recovery requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: identity-mismatch handling and fraud-sensitive guest communication escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Are call center environments considered sensitive areas for PCI DSS Requirement 9.1.1?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body, and this FAQ directly clarifies that call centers handling cardholder data require controlled access because they process or transmit payment data.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment-data handling in contact-center or messaging-assisted guest support

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and authority grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm channel, property, guest identity, reservation or folio references, and whether the request is in scope, blocked, or owned by another lane before responding.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Reservation, folio, and message-record review
workflow_stages.description: Validate reservation state, room type, fee presentation, payment history, prior contacts, disruption notes, and service-recovery approvals so the answer stays tied to authoritative records.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Supported guest communication and expectation control
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence-backed guest messaging that explains what is known, what is approved, what remains open, and which owner must act next without overstating authority or finality.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Disruption, stay, and recovery communication handling
workflow_stages.description: Use approved outage notices, accessibility-aware communication, and delegated recovery messaging when tenant policy and authoritative records explicitly support the communication.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Policy-exception, repeat-failure, and payment-conflict escalation
workflow_stages.description: Move unsupported compensation asks, fee disputes, folio mismatches, repeat failures, and policy conflicts into explicit escalation packets with the correct owner and evidence set.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Safety, public-health, fraud, and payment-security stop-condition handling
workflow_stages.description: Stop unsupported actions and escalate whenever outbreak exposure, security concern, suspicious identity, or payment-data handling signals change the safe answer.
source_classes: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: reservation confirmations, confirmation numbers, room-type assignments, and cancellation terms
PMS folio snapshots, incidental holds, payment-tender history, refund status, and fee displays
guest profiles, preference notes, accessible-room notes, and prior contact history
contact-center transcripts, messaging threads, complaint logs, and service tickets
service-recovery matrices, approved recovery offers, escalation notes, and repeat-failure histories
outage notices, room out-of-order logs, maintenance or transportation disruption notes, and incident reports
guest lists, safety or public-health notices, fraud or loss-prevention notes, and escalation records

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked, complaint-driven, disruption-related, or policy-exception-sensitive.
Request the minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed to continue safely.
Provide evidence-backed communication summaries, status explanations, and next-step packaging without overstating authority.
Prepare escalation packets and handoff summaries when ownership shifts.
escalate: Required reservation, folio, payment, service-recovery, accessibility, or incident records are missing or materially conflict.
The request requires room-control, pricing override, refund exception approval, compensation approval, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, or safety signoff.
An outbreak, safety, suspicious identity, or payment-data-handling signal changes whether direct guest communication can proceed.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, brand, supplier, processor, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
refuse: Promise a refund, fee waiver, upgrade, or compensation action that the authoritative record and policy set do not support.
Override pricing, refund, safety, accessibility, or fraud controls without explicit authority.
Ask for or restate full payment-card data in insecure communication channels.

domain_failure_modes: the lane promises a refund, waived fee, room move, or recovery offer when the folio, policy, or approval map does not support it
a reservation or folio mismatch is handled as generic hospitality tone instead of being routed into the correct front-office, revenue, or issuer path
a guest communication closes without the minimum reservation, property, stay, identity, or payment evidence
an accessibility-sensitive request is answered without enough detail about room features, alternate formats, or actual inventory constraints
repeat failures are treated as ordinary apology cases instead of escalation-worthy complaint patterns
outbreak, safety, or fraud indicators are ignored in the name of guest appeasement
the industry overlay collapses into generic customer-service language instead of hospitality reservation, folio, outage, and service-recovery terminology

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific lodging taxes, resort-fee disclosure rules beyond the FTC baseline, and property-specific supplier or package terms can materially narrow the guest-facing answer.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve tenant jurisdiction, property terms, supplier contracts, and brand fee scripts before applying property-specific outcomes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Public-health messaging, emergency notices, and accessibility accommodations may require local legal, compliance, or property review before outbound messaging is sent.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane records the ambiguity and routes to the designated safety, compliance, or front-office owner instead of guessing. No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains because the escalation path is explicit.

notes: The research baseline covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure behavior instead of only generic guest-service scripting.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated packaging because jurisdictional, property, brand, supplier, and processor differences are explicitly handled through runtime retrieval and escalation rules.

The lane stops at guest communication, expectation control, and delegated messaging. Pricing overrides, discretionary compensation approval, room-control decisions, fraud findings, and safety authority remain outside the lane.
